mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly increases in auto sales and housing starts reflect synchronized consumer demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a directional accuracy below 55% over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index above 105 signals robust manufacturing capacity utilization that supports near-term earnings revisions, yielding positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of observations where INDPRO exceeds 105 during mid-cycle expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 238.0 indicates accelerating price pressures that force equity valuation compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL crosses the 238.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms genuine labor market tightening, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter through earnings growth.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to generate positive 60-day SPX returns with a hit rate below 52% over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity that translates into positive corporate earnings surprises, pushing S&P 500 returns higher within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 100 during a recessionary phase, it confirms severe output contraction that forces equity deleveraging, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in > 65% of observations where INDPRO drops below 100 during confirmed recessionary periods.
